name: Crouching Lion Trail
tagline: Short but steep scramble to a dramatic rock formation with panoramic views of Kahana Bay and the Ko'olau range.
parking: Small pullout on Kamehameha Hwy near mile marker 27. Limited to 5-6 cars
bestTime: Early morning for cooler temps and better light. Trail gets full sun with no shade
talkStory: In Hawaiian legend this rock is Kauhi, a demigod turned to stone by Hi'iaka (Pele's sister) during her epic journey across the islands — the 'crouching lion' name came later from Westerners who saw a different shape. The trail is unofficial and unmaintained with serious drop-offs near the summit, so treat it with respect. Locals approach from the old Crouching Lion Inn restaurant side, not directly from the highway. The 360-degree panorama of Kahana Bay and the Ko'olau knife-edge ridgeline rewards a short but intense scramble.
